cards.back: A concept in game theory that describes a situation where no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy, assuming all other players keep their strategies unchanged.
It ensures that no player has an incentive to change their strategy as long as the other players keep theirs the same.

cards.front: Who introduced the concept of Nash equilibrium?
cards.back: John Nash, an American mathematician and economist, introduced the concept of Nash equilibrium in the 1950s.

cards.front: What is the main characteristic of a Nash equilibrium?
cards.back: The main characteristic is that it is a stable state where no player can improve their outcome by deviating from their chosen strategy.

cards.front: Is Nash equilibrium a Pareto optimal outcome?
cards.back: No, it is not necessarily a Pareto optimal outcome. Players may not be able to achieve the best possible outcome for themselves and the other players simultaneously.
